'''David Hodgson''' (born 8 August 1981) is an English professional [[rugby league]] [[Coach (sport)|coach]] who is an assistant coach at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and a former [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. 

He played at international level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]], as well as representing [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]]. At club level for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], the [[Wigan Warriors]],<ref name="Heritage Numbers - In Debut Order">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|title=Heritage Numbers - In Debut Order|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2019|access-date=1 January 2020|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327102545/http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|url-status=dead}}</ref> the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] (two spells), as an occasional [[Goal (sports)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|fb|wg}}, or {{rlp|ce}},<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and has coached at club level for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] (assistant).<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
===Halifax===
Born in [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]], Hodgson began his career at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in 1999, before moving to the Wigan Warriors in 2000, a tribunal fixing a £45,000 fee.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Halifax call for action over `Wigan poachers' |url=https://www.the-independent.com/sport/rugby-league-halifax-call-for-action-over-wigan-poachers-1105075.html |website=The Independent |access-date=29 October 2024 |date=7 July 1999}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Young Britain show their age |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-young-britain-show-their-age-1131747.html |website=The Independent |access-date=29 October 2024 |date=11 December 1999}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
Hodgson played on the {{rlp|wg}} and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in the [[Wigan Warriors]]' 16-29 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] during 2000's [[Super League V]] at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Saturday 14 October 2000, in front of a crowd of 58,132.

In 2003 he was the Wigan Warriors's top [[Try (rugby)|try]] scorer with 20. He played for the Wigan Warriors at {{rlp|ce}} in the [[2003 Super League Grand Final]] which was lost to the [[Bradford Bulls]].

He signed for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] from the Wigan Warriors and when his contract expired at the end of 2004.<ref>{{cite web |title=Salford sign Wigan duo |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/salford/3583362.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=29 October 2024 |date=20 August 2004}}</ref> He made a total of 118 appearances for Wigan, in which time he scored 48 tries.

===Salford City Reds===
Hodgson regained full fitness at the Salford City Reds which saw him named in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] standby squad for the [[Rugby League Tri-Nations|Tri-Nations]], the engage [[Super League Dream Team]] and also being named Supporter's Player of the Year and Player's Player of the Year at the Salford City Reds' annual awards ceremony. The club finished fifth in 2006's [[Super League XI]], their highest ever position, and went on to the end of season [[play-off]]s for the first time.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

On 11 March 2006, he scored a club [[Super League]] record 8-[[Goal (sports)#Rugby football|goal]] kicks – despite having never kicked a goal before the start of 2006's [[Super League XI]].
[[File:David Hodgson.jpg|thumb|right|Hodgson in action for the Huddersfield Giants]]

===Huddersfield Giants===
In September 2007 Hodgson signed for the Huddersfield Giants, following the Salford City Reds' relegation from Super League.<ref>{{cite web |title=Hodgson signs for Giants |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/2754917/hodgson-signs-for-giants |website=Sky Sports |access-date=29 October 2024 |date=25 September 2007}}</ref> He missed most of the [[2008 Super League season|2008 season]] after suffering a knee ligament injury in training.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Barraclough |first1=Neil |title=Huddersfield's new signing Hodgson out for six months |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/jan/11/rugbyleague.sport |website=The Guardian |access-date=29 October 2024 |date=11 January 2008}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 18 July 2011, Hodgson signed a three-year deal with his hometown club, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref name="Huddersfield Giants man David Hodgson to join Hull KR">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14186973|title=Huddersfield Giants man David Hodgson to join Hull KR|publisher=BBC|date=18 July 2011|access-date=18 July 2011}}</ref>

==Representative==
On 10 November Hodgson made his first appearance of [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]]'s test series against New Zealand and scored.

In June 2007 he was called up to the Great Britain squad for the [[Test match (rugby league)|Test match]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6744909.stm|title=Eight new faces in Lions squad|publisher=BBC|date=12 June 2007|access-date=12 June 2007}}</ref>

He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Myler gets England call">{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|title=Myler gets England call|publisher=England Rugby League|date=29 September 2008|access-date=3 October 2008|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|archive-date=7 October 2008|df=dmy }}</ref>

He was named in the [[England national rugby league team|England]] team to face [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]] prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Gleeson to lead new-look England">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Gleeson to lead new-look England|publisher=BBC|date=9 October 2008|access-date=10 October 2008}}</ref>

==Post-playing career==
On 3 October 2014, Hodgson announced his retirement and he signed a two-year contract to stay at his hometown club [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] as an assistant coach, along with [[Willie Poching]].<ref name="Hodgson To Take Coaching Role">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/31216/hodgson-to-take-coaching-role|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141004065628/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/31216/hodgson-to-take-coaching-role|url-status=dead|archive-date=4 October 2014|title=Hodgson To Take Coaching Role (archived by web.archive.org)|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=3 October 2014|access-date=3 October 2014}}</ref>
